description: Make update's per-component partial application legible in one place: UpdateResult gains an optional partialApplication field — populated exactly when the run both refused something (blocked, refused, or conflicts non-empty) AND applied something (resynced, migrated, merged, migrationsRegistered, or any registryAdditions array non-empty) — carrying applied {resynced, migrated, merged, registryAdditions, migrationsRegistered}, notApplied {blocked, refused, conflicts}, and a one-line legible summary naming what was applied alongside what was refused and why, so a blocked run never reads as a no-op. Computed for live and dryRun runs (dryRun reports the predicted partiality). The update op's description/promptSnippet state the behavior.
acceptance_criteria: A live update that blocks (or refuses/conflicts) at least one schema while applying registry additions or other-schema resyncs returns partialApplication with both sides populated and a summary naming the blocked schema(s), the refusal reason, and the applied categories.
A fully-clean run (nothing refused, or nothing applied) carries no partialApplication field; existing result channels are unchanged.
--dryRun over the same fixture reports the predicted partiality in the same shape, writing nothing.
The update op's description and promptSnippet state the partial-application reporting; build, check, and the full test suite pass at zero failures with the change in dist/.
notes: Closes the update-result legibility gap (its filed resolution verbatim); the milestone's last open member.
